LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ALLERGAN, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT 1 (PMA LETTER). THE FDA REQUIRED DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE TRAINING TO PHYSICIANS. PARS. 19 AND 20 OF THE 2ND AC DEMONSTRATE THAT THE TRAINING WAS PROVIDED. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGING THAT THE TRAINING WAS INSUFFICIENT AND SOMETHING MORE WAS NECESSARY, FEDERAL LAW EXPRESSLY PREEMPTS THE CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF RELIES ON VIOLATIONS OF FEDERAL REGULATIONS WITHOUT CITING A STATE-LAW DUTY THAT PARALLELS A FEDERAL-LAW DUTY, THE CLAIM IS IMPLIEDLY PREEMPTED. SEE STENGEL V. MEDTRONIC, INC. 704 F.3D 1224, 1230 (9TH CIR. 2013). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MITCHEL J. OLSON, TEL. NO. (760) 634-6755 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JAN DODD OF THE NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 892-9200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ALLERGAN INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ALLERGAN, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of exhibit 1 (PMA letter). The FDA required defendant to provide training to physicians. Pars. 19 and 20 of the 2nd AC demonstrate that the training was provided. To the extent plaintiff is alleging that the training was insufficient and something more was necessary, federal law expressly preempts the claim. To the extent plaintiff relies on violations of federal regulations without citing a state-law duty that parallels a federal-law duty, the claim is impliedly preempted. See Stengel v. Medtronic, Inc. 704 F.3d 1224, 1230 (9th Cir. 2013). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ASHLEY GLENNEN's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. Allowing the amendment is not prejudicial. Defendant does not conclusively demonstrate that the new claim fails as a matter of law and the better practice is to have those issues raised by demurrer or a motion for summary judgment. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
